title: Laboratory Observation
description: Usually in-situ observations in which the Object or Phenomenon is isolated from other systems or altered in its original conditions or environment.

definitions.definition: Usually in-situ observations in which the Object or Phenomenon is isolated from other systems or altered in its original conditions or environment.
definitions.notes: - It is important to consider that Laboratory Observations are typically conducted in controlled conditions, allowing for isolation or alteration of the Object or Phenomenon from its natural environment or other systems.
